item.title: dice total
item.description:
A dice total in dice games is the sum of the face values shown after a roll [@britannica2026craps; @britannica2026backgammon].

Dice totals matter because many games read combinations through addition rather than through each die separately. That simple arithmetic creates the probability structure players learn to anticipate: some totals appear more often than others, and the game can attach meaning to that pattern.

item.title: pip
item.description:
A pip in dice games is one of the spots marking value on a die face, and by extension the face value those spots represent [@britannica2026dice; @britannica2026backgammon].

Pips matter because they make random outcomes machine-readable for players. A die is useful only because the marks on its faces can be counted quickly and translated into movement, sums, or other consequences under the rules.

item.title: roll
item.description:
A roll in dice games is the cast of one or more dice that generates the current outcome [@britannica2026dice; @britannica2026craps].

The roll matters because it is the public event around which the rest of the game turns. In some games the roll directly settles success or failure. In others it generates movement, scoring options, or wagering consequences that players must then interpret.
